title: Connecticut Meal Break Requirements
summary: Connecticut requires employers to provide a 30-minute meal break to employees who work a shift of 7.5 or more consecutive hours. The meal break must be provided after the first 2 hours of work and before the last 2 hours of work. The break may be unpaid if the employee is completely relieved of all duties. Employers in certain industries may apply to the Labor Commissioner for exemptions.

meal_period.timing: Must be provided after the first 2 hours of work and before the last 2 hours of work.
meal_period.description: A 30-minute meal break must be provided to employees who work shifts of 7.5 or more consecutive hours. The break must fall after the first 2 hours and before the last 2 hours of the shift.

exemptions.commissioner_exemption: Employers in certain industries may apply to the Labor Commissioner for an exemption from the meal break requirement if compliance is impractical due to the nature of the business.
exemptions.compliance_conditions: Employers who allow employees to eat while working may satisfy the requirement if the employee is compensated for the time.

penalties.description: Employers who violate the meal break requirement may be subject to fines imposed by the Connecticut Department of Labor. Employees may file complaints with the department for enforcement.

notes: Connecticut's meal break law is more specific than many states in requiring the break to fall within a defined window of the shift (after the first 2 hours and before the last 2 hours). The law applies broadly across industries but allows for exemptions by application to the Labor Commissioner.
